Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe connected accounts using stripe_list_connected_accounts to identify Marcus Webb's departed LO commission account. The agent needs to discover account acct_scenario_644_departed with email marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com, business_name "Marcus Webb Consulting," metadata showing staff_id los_staff_a583f044387a and departed date 2026-03-15, and that charges_enabled and payouts_enabled are still active despite his departure.
OE 2: List Stripe transfers filtered to Marcus Webb's connected account using stripe_list_transfers (with destination_account_id: acct_scenario_644_departed, or unfiltered and then identifying Webb transfers) to find all post-departure commission disbursements. The agent needs to discover 5 LO commission transfers: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250 Webb/Chen, LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875 Webb/Patel, LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980 Webb/Torres, LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450 Webb/Kim, LN-2026-00211), and tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100 Webb/Okafor, LN-2026-00608), all routed to acct_scenario_644_departed, totaling $7,655.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Danielle Webb" or "Marcus Webb" or "conflict" or "disclosure" (or similar) to discover which loan files Denise previously flagged for the undisclosed spouse-agent relationship. The agent needs to find Denise's escalation email (email_email_87f2ae2b3a67, subject "Potential undisclosed conflict review - Marcus Webb files") identifying Danielle Webb as buyer's agent on LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Danielle Webb" or "Marcus" or "conflict" or "commission" (or similar) to find internal discussions confirming Danielle Webb's involvement as buyer's agent on Marcus's files. The agent needs to discover: (a) Denise's message in #loan-processing (C002) identifying Danielle Webb as Marcus's wife via LinkedIn and flagging loans LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227, (b) Grace's confirmation in #loan-processing (C002) of sending Marcus the disclosure request, and (c) Denise's more detailed sensitive flagging in a DM to Grace (channel D_denise_grace) raising the undisclosed conflict concern and specifically noting unease about LN-2026-00211 with the appraisal gap.
OE 5: Search the Mortgage LOS for the 5 loan files referenced in the commission transfer descriptions using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (with queries or loan_numbers: LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00608) to retrieve loan details. The agent will find that the current assigned_lo on these loans is no longer Marcus Webb (loans were reassigned to other LOs such as Derek Moss, James Thornton, and Brian Mitchell after Marcus's March 15 departure), but can confirm Marcus was the originating LO by matching the Stripe transfer descriptions ("LO commission Webb") and the connected account metadata (staff_id los_staff_a583f044387a). Also search for the additional Danielle Webb flagged loans LN-2026-00629 and LN-2026-00227 to build the complete picture. The agent needs to cross-reference these two sets and discover that LN-2026-00211 appears in both the commission transfer list (Stripe description "Webb/Kim," $1,450) and the Danielle Webb flagged list (identified as a Danielle Webb buyer's-agent file per email and Slack evidence in OE 3 and OE 4). Note: the LOS borrower for LN-2026-00211 is Elizabeth Gray the "Kim" label appears only in the Stripe transfer description and the LOS realtor_name field shows Elizabeth Rogers rather than Danielle Webb; Danielle's role as buyer's agent on these files is established through email and Slack discussions, not the LOS realtor field. This is the critical overlap where commissions flowed to Marcus on a transaction where his wife was identified as the buyer's agent.
OE 6: Pull conditions for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_get_conditions (with loan_id for each affected loan, at minimum the overlap loan LN-2026-00211 and the other Danielle Webb flagged loans LN-2026-00629 and LN-2026-00227). The agent needs to retrieve condition records to understand the compliance and underwriting status of each affected file.
OE 7: Pull borrower details for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_search_borrowers or mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id (with borrower_ids from the loan records). The agent needs to retrieve borrower information (names, contact details, loan status) for each affected loan to build the full compliance picture.
OE 8: Pull document checklists for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_get_documents (with loan_id for each affected loan). The agent needs to discover document checklist items (URLA, credit reports, W-2s, appraisal, title commitment, etc.) and their statuses for each affected file.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "written disclosure" or "Webb files" or "explanation" (or similar) to find Grace Yamamoto's request to Marcus for a written explanation and Marcus's response. The agent needs to discover: (a) Grace's email (email_email_07784db05b1d) to marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com, CC'd to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, requesting written disclosure and explanation on LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227 with a deadline of 3:00 PM, and (b) Marcus's reply (email_email_24f2ba35c614) which disputes the framing and references Pine Hollow and appraisal-gap options rather than providing a compliant disclosure.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Laura Bennett" or "ethics" or "conflict" or "bennettethicslaw" (or similar) to check whether Laura Bennett has responded on the conflict question. The agent needs to discover: (a) Denise's outbound email to laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (email_email_5afe8e8b1a88) requesting ethics guidance on the undisclosed spouse-agent arrangement, and (b) Denise's outbound email to laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (email_email_bde65cc3733a) regarding the departing LO situation and non-compete. Critically, the agent must determine that NO inbound reply from Laura Bennett exists in the mailbox Laura has not responded yet.
OE 11: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find compliance follow-up notes related to the Marcus Webb conflict. The agent needs to discover engagement crm_engagement_9f65d4c5063d ("Grace called Laura Bennett re departing LO" with NC non-compete details), engagement crm_engagement_ec52b5bd43bd (spouse concern flagging loans LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227), and related engagement notes documenting the compliance investigation trail.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Laura Bennett using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Laura Bennett" or similar) to identify the correct email address for sending the payment breakdown. The agent needs to discover that multiple Laura Bennett contacts exist: laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (Ethics Counsel, contacts_contact_685aa7cf1577) and laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (Employment Counsel, contacts_contact_8fbcc2da45b5). The agent must determine which Laura Bennett to email the ethics counsel at bennettethicslaw.com is the one handling the conflict-of-interest question specifically, while the employment counsel at bennettstokeslaw.com was consulted on the departure/non-compete issue. The payment breakdown with overlap analysis relates to the conflict question, so laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com is the most appropriate recipient, though laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com is also a valid recipient given the financial overlap implicates both the ethics and employment dimensions.
OE 13: Look up Robert Calloway's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert" or "Calloway" or similar) to identify the correct email address for briefing him on combined exposure. The agent needs to discover r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (contact_id staff_e85bc913c756, job "Broker Keystone Mortgage Partners"). Note: the email system also uses robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com as sender/recipient across existing correspondence, so either address variant is valid for sending.
OE 14: Identify the compliance-alerts Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" or similar) to find the channel_id for posting the heads-up. The agent needs to discover that #compliance-alerts is channel C004.
OE 15: Lock every affected loan file by updating each loan's status and/or notes to reflect a compliance hold using mortgage_los_update_loan for each affected loan. The affected loans include at minimum: LN-2026-00211 (overlap both post-departure commission and Danielle Webb as agent), LN-2026-00629 (Danielle Webb as agent), and LN-2026-00227 (Danielle Webb as agent). The remaining commission-only loans (LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00608) should also be locked for the post-departure commission routing violation. Each update must include the specific violation documented in the notes field (e.g., undisclosed conflict of interest, post-departure commission routing, or both for LN-2026-00211).
OE 16: Add a note to every affected loan's activity log using mortgage_los_add_activity for each affected loan. The activity entries should document the specific violation found (post-departure commission routing to departed LO Marcus Webb, undisclosed spouse-agent conflict with Danielle Webb, or combined violation for the overlap loan LN-2026-00211). Parameters: loan_id (for each affected loan), action (e.g., "compliance_hold" or "note" or similar), detail (specific violation description including relevant amounts, dates, and parties).
OE 17: Send an email to Laura Bennett using send_email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and recipient laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (or laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com) containing the payment breakdown and overlap analysis. The email must include: (a) the 5 post-departure commission transfers with amounts and loan numbers (totaling $7,655), (b) the overlap analysis showing LN-2026-00211 (borrower Elizabeth Gray; Stripe description "Webb/Kim") has both a $1,450 commission payment to Marcus AND Danielle Webb identified as buyer's agent per internal email/Slack findings, (c) the regulatory implications (disclosure issue now compounded by a financial conflict), and (d) request for updated guidance given the financial dimension.
OE 18: Send an email to Robert Calloway using send_email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and recipient r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, both valid) briefing him on the combined exposure. The email must include: (a) summary of post-departure commission payments still routing to Marcus's account ($7,655 total), (b) the overlap with the Danielle Webb conflict-of-interest files (LN-2026-00211), (c) the status of Grace's request for Marcus's written explanation (received a non-compliant response disputing the framing), (d) Laura Bennett's response status (no reply yet from either ethics or employment counsel), and (e) actions taken (loan files locked, activity logs updated, Laura emailed with full breakdown).
OE 19: Post a heads-up message in #compliance-alerts using conversations_add_message with channel_id C004. The message should alert the compliance team to the Marcus Webb post-departure commission issue and its overlap with the previously flagged Danielle Webb conflict-of-interest files, noting that affected loan files have been locked and outside counsel has been updated.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe connected accounts using stripe_list_connected_accounts to identify Marcus Webb's departed LO commission account. The agent needs to discover account acct_scenario_644_departed with email marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com, business_name "Marcus Webb Consulting," metadata showing staff_id los_staff_a583f044387a and departed date 2026-03-15, and that charges_enabled and payouts_enabled are still active despite his departure.
OE 2: List Stripe transfers filtered to Marcus Webb's connected account using stripe_list_transfers (with destination_account_id: acct_scenario_644_departed, or unfiltered and then identifying Webb transfers) to find all post-departure commission disbursements. The agent needs to discover 5 LO commission transfers: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250 Webb/Chen, LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875 Webb/Patel, LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980 Webb/Torres, LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450 Webb/Kim, LN-2026-00211), and tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100 Webb/Okafor, LN-2026-00608), all routed to acct_scenario_644_departed, totaling $7,655.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Danielle Webb" or "Marcus Webb" or "conflict" or "disclosure" (or similar) to discover which loan files Denise previously flagged for the undisclosed spouse-agent relationship. The agent needs to find Denise's escalation email (email_email_87f2ae2b3a67, subject "Potential undisclosed conflict review - Marcus Webb files") identifying Danielle Webb as buyer's agent on LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Danielle Webb" or "Marcus" or "conflict" or "commission" (or similar) to find internal discussions confirming Danielle Webb's involvement as buyer's agent on Marcus's files. The agent needs to discover: (a) Denise's message in #loan-processing (C002) identifying Danielle Webb as Marcus's wife via LinkedIn and flagging loans LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227, (b) Grace's confirmation in #loan-processing (C002) of sending Marcus the disclosure request, and (c) Denise's more detailed sensitive flagging in a DM to Grace (channel D_denise_grace) raising the undisclosed conflict concern and specifically noting unease about LN-2026-00211 with the appraisal gap.
OE 5: Search the Mortgage LOS for the 5 loan files referenced in the commission transfer descriptions using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (with queries or loan_numbers: LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00608) to retrieve loan details. The agent will find that the current assigned_lo on these loans is no longer Marcus Webb (loans were reassigned to other LOs such as Derek Moss, James Thornton, and Brian Mitchell after Marcus's March 15 departure), but can confirm Marcus was the originating LO by matching the Stripe transfer descriptions ("LO commission Webb") and the connected account metadata (staff_id los_staff_a583f044387a). Also search for the additional Danielle Webb flagged loans LN-2026-00629 and LN-2026-00227 to build the complete picture. The agent needs to cross-reference these two sets and discover that LN-2026-00211 appears in both the commission transfer list (Stripe description "Webb/Kim," $1,450) and the Danielle Webb flagged list (identified as a Danielle Webb buyer's-agent file per email and Slack evidence in OE 3 and OE 4). Note: the LOS borrower for LN-2026-00211 is Elizabeth Gray the "Kim" label appears only in the Stripe transfer description and the LOS realtor_name field shows Elizabeth Rogers rather than Danielle Webb; Danielle's role as buyer's agent on these files is established through email and Slack discussions, not the LOS realtor field. This is the critical overlap where commissions flowed to Marcus on a transaction where his wife was identified as the buyer's agent.
OE 6: Pull conditions for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_get_conditions (with loan_id for each affected loan, at minimum the overlap loan LN-2026-00211 and the other Danielle Webb flagged loans LN-2026-00629 and LN-2026-00227). The agent needs to retrieve condition records to understand the compliance and underwriting status of each affected file.
OE 7: Pull borrower details for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_search_borrowers or mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id (with borrower_ids from the loan records). The agent needs to retrieve borrower information (names, contact details, loan status) for each affected loan to build the full compliance picture.
OE 8: Pull document checklists for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_get_documents (with loan_id for each affected loan). The agent needs to discover document checklist items (URLA, credit reports, W-2s, appraisal, title commitment, etc.) and their statuses for each affected file.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "written disclosure" or "Webb files" or "explanation" (or similar) to find Grace Yamamoto's request to Marcus for a written explanation and Marcus's response. The agent needs to discover: (a) Grace's email (email_email_07784db05b1d) to marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com, CC'd to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, requesting written disclosure and explanation on LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227 with a deadline of 3:00 PM, and (b) Marcus's reply (email_email_24f2ba35c614) which disputes the framing and references Pine Hollow and appraisal-gap options rather than providing a compliant disclosure.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Laura Bennett" or "ethics" or "conflict" or "bennettethicslaw" (or similar) to check whether Laura Bennett has responded on the conflict question. The agent needs to discover: (a) Denise's outbound email to laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (email_email_5afe8e8b1a88) requesting ethics guidance on the undisclosed spouse-agent arrangement, and (b) Denise's outbound email to laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (email_email_bde65cc3733a) regarding the departing LO situation and non-compete. Critically, the agent must determine that NO inbound reply from Laura Bennett exists in the mailbox Laura has not responded yet.
OE 11: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find compliance follow-up notes related to the Marcus Webb conflict. The agent needs to discover engagement crm_engagement_9f65d4c5063d ("Grace called Laura Bennett re departing LO" with NC non-compete details), engagement crm_engagement_ec52b5bd43bd (spouse concern flagging loans LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227), and related engagement notes documenting the compliance investigation trail.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Laura Bennett using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Laura Bennett" or similar) to identify the correct email address for sending the payment breakdown. The agent needs to discover that multiple Laura Bennett contacts exist: laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (Ethics Counsel, contacts_contact_685aa7cf1577) and laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (Employment Counsel, contacts_contact_8fbcc2da45b5). The agent must determine which Laura Bennett to email the ethics counsel at bennettethicslaw.com is the one handling the conflict-of-interest question specifically, while the employment counsel at bennettstokeslaw.com was consulted on the departure/non-compete issue. The payment breakdown with overlap analysis relates to the conflict question, so laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com is the most appropriate recipient, though laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com is also a valid recipient given the financial overlap implicates both the ethics and employment dimensions.
OE 13: Look up Robert Calloway's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert" or "Calloway" or similar) to identify the correct email address for briefing him on combined exposure. The agent needs to discover r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (contact_id staff_e85bc913c756, job "Broker Keystone Mortgage Partners"). Note: the email system also uses robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com as sender/recipient across existing correspondence, so either address variant is valid for sending.
OE 14: Identify the compliance-alerts Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" or similar) to find the channel_id for posting the heads-up. The agent needs to discover that #compliance-alerts is channel C004.
OE 15: Lock every affected loan file by updating each loan's status and/or notes to reflect a compliance hold using mortgage_los_update_loan for each affected loan. The affected loans include at minimum: LN-2026-00211 (overlap both post-departure commission and Danielle Webb as agent), LN-2026-00629 (Danielle Webb as agent), and LN-2026-00227 (Danielle Webb as agent). The remaining commission-only loans (LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00608) should also be locked for the post-departure commission routing violation. Each update must include the specific violation documented in the notes field (e.g., undisclosed conflict of interest, post-departure commission routing, or both for LN-2026-00211).
OE 16: Add a note to every affected loan's activity log using mortgage_los_add_activity for each affected loan. The activity entries should document the specific violation found (post-departure commission routing to departed LO Marcus Webb, undisclosed spouse-agent conflict with Danielle Webb, or combined violation for the overlap loan LN-2026-00211). Parameters: loan_id (for each affected loan), action (e.g., "compliance_hold" or "note" or similar), detail (specific violation description including relevant amounts, dates, and parties).
OE 17: Send an email to Laura Bennett using send_email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and recipient laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com (or laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com) containing the payment breakdown and overlap analysis. The email must include: (a) the 5 post-departure commission transfers with amounts and loan numbers (totaling $7,655), (b) the overlap analysis showing LN-2026-00211 (borrower Elizabeth Gray; Stripe description "Webb/Kim") has both a $1,450 commission payment to Marcus AND Danielle Webb identified as buyer's agent per internal email/Slack findings, (c) the regulatory implications (disclosure issue now compounded by a financial conflict), and (d) request for updated guidance given the financial dimension.
OE 18: Send an email to Robert Calloway using send_email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and recipient r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, both valid) briefing him on the combined exposure. The email must include: (a) summary of post-departure commission payments still routing to Marcus's account ($7,655 total), (b) the overlap with the Danielle Webb conflict-of-interest files (LN-2026-00211), (c) the status of Grace's request for Marcus's written explanation (received a non-compliant response disputing the framing), (d) Laura Bennett's response status (no reply yet from either ethics or employment counsel), and (e) actions taken (loan files locked, activity logs updated, Laura emailed with full breakdown).
OE 19: Post a heads-up message in #compliance-alerts using conversations_add_message with channel_id C004. The message should alert the compliance team to the Marcus Webb post-departure commission issue and its overlap with the previously flagged Danielle Webb conflict-of-interest files, noting that affected loan files have been locked and outside counsel has been updated.

1 The model must use a Stripe tool (e.g., stripe_list_connected_accounts, stripe_list_transfers, or stripe_get_transfer) to investigate commission disbursements to Marcus Webb's account.
tool selection

✓Run #6The agent called both stripe_list_connected_accounts (to identify Marcus Webb's connected account acct_scenario_644_departed) and stripe_list_transfers (with destination_account_id: 'acct_scenario_644_departed') to retrieve all 5 post-departure commission transfers totaling $7,655.

4 Loan LN-2026-00211 was updated (via mortgage_los_update_loan) to a compliance hold or locked status (e.g., 'withdrawn,' 'on_hold,' 'suspended,' or similar), with the notes field documenting both the post-departure commission routing to Marcus Webb and the undisclosed Danielle Webb spouse-agent conflict (or similar dual violation description).

The loan was already in withdrawn status and the notes document both violations.

✓Run #3The final response explicitly identifies LN-2026-00211 (borrower Elizabeth Gray) as 'THE OVERLAP FILE: $1,450 commission paid to Marcus on this file where his wife was the agent' and labels it 'The nexus: a departed loan officer collected commission on a transaction where his spouse was the buyer's agent.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly identifies LN-2026-00211 as 'HIGHEST RISK' and states 'Danielle was buyer's agent + Marcus received $1,450 commission = dual-sided household compensation with no disclosure,' directly identifying it as the critical overlap.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies LN-2026-00211 (Elizabeth Gray, FHA, $217K) as the direct overlap: 'Danielle as buyer's agent + $1,450 commission paid to Marcus = same household earning on both sides,' and calls it 'the direct RESPA Section 8 exposure.'
✓Run #6The final response identifies LN-2026-00211 as 'HIGHEST RISK' and explicitly states '$1,450 commission paid to Marcus's account = direct financial conflict' with Danielle Webb as buyer's agent, correctly identifying it as the critical overlap loan.
31 The final response confirms that Laura Bennett has not responded to the conflict-of-interest inquiry.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states 'Laura Bennett has not yet responded to the conflict inquiry,' confirming no response was received from outside counsel.
✓Run #2The final response states 'Laura has NOT responded to the ethics consultation I sent her on the spouse-agent conflict' and notes she also hasn't responded to the separate departing-LO consultation, clearly confirming no response was received.
✓Run #3The final response includes a 'Laura Bennett Response' section explicitly stating 'No response received' and explaining that a note was sent but 'no reply has come back on the conflict question.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'I sent Laura the initial ethics inquiry. No response received yet.' confirming Laura Bennett has not responded to the conflict-of-interest inquiry.
✓Run #5The final response states 'She has NOT responded yet' regarding Laura Bennett's response to the conflict question, consistent with the correct finding from the email search results.
✓Run #6The final response states 'Laura Bennett has not yet responded to the ethics/disclosure inquiry I sent her on the three Danielle Webb files,' clearly confirming no response was received from counsel.
32 The final response reports that Marcus Webb's written explanation to Grace Yamamoto was non-compliant: he disputed the framing of the conflict and did not provide a proper affiliated business arrangement disclosure (or similar characterization of an inadequate response).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1The final response characterizes Marcus's response as: 'dismissed the concern as "not illegal in NC," did not address disclosure gap.' The email to Laura also states he 'characterized the concern as overstated' and 'did not address the non-disclosure issue at all' — clearly portraying an evasive, non-compliant response.
✓Run #2The final response characterizes Marcus's response as 'dismissive' — he 'claimed the arrangement is not illegal in NC but did not address why the relationship was never disclosed,' did not confirm borrower notification, denied compensation arrangement, and did not address the appraisal gap conflict. This clearly describes a non-compliant, evasive response.
✗Run #3The final response states 'No response received' regarding Marcus's written explanation — it says 'there is no evidence Marcus ever provided a written explanation.' The agent did not find Marcus's actual reply (email_email_24f2ba35c614) in which he disputed the framing and deflected. The correct finding was that Marcus gave a non-compliant response disputing the conflict characterization, not that he gave no response at all.
✓Run #4The final response quotes Marcus's pushback directly ('Danielle being my wife does not make these transactions illegal in North Carolina' and 'I do not agree that this should be treated like some kind of ethics event') and characterizes his response as non-compliant, stating 'He does not acknowledge any disclosure obligation.'
✗Run #5The agent reported that 'Marcus has NOT provided a written response' — presenting the situation as no response at all. Per the criterion, Marcus did respond to Grace's request (email_email_24f2ba35c614) but in a non-compliant, evasive manner. The agent failed to find Marcus's actual reply email and thus missed characterizing it as an inadequate/non-compliant response.
✗Run #6The agent reported 'no written explanation from Marcus was received before his departure,' stating there was no response at all. The criteria requires characterizing Marcus's actual reply as non-compliant/dismissive (he disputed the framing and referenced appraisal-gap options rather than addressing disclosure). The agent failed to locate Marcus's reply email (email_email_24f2ba35c614) through its searches, so it incorrectly concluded no response existed rather than finding and characterizing it as inadequate.
Total 29/3223/3221/3223/3221/3221/32 72%
